rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Drain All Contract Funds via superWithdraw
rejected_candidates.reason: Privileged `onlyOwner` emergency withdrawal capability; this is trust/governance centralization, not an unauthorized exploit path.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Set Malicious DODORouteProxy to Steal User Funds
rejected_candidates.reason: Depends on malicious owner action; treated as admin trust assumption rather than a permissionless vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Set Malicious DODOApprove to Steal User Tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: Depends on malicious owner action; not a non-privileged exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Set Excessive FeePercent Degrading User Swaps
rejected_candidates.reason: Owner-governed parameter risk; policy/centralization issue, not a code-level exploit by external attackers.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Set Excessive Slippage Causing User Loss
rejected_candidates.reason: Owner-controlled configuration risk rather than unauthorized exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: Arbitrary Gateway Address Can Be Set by Owner
rejected_candidates.reason: Admin trust assumption; requires privileged owner compromise or malicious governance.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Deadline Validation in Uniswap Swaps
rejected_candidates.reason: No concrete theft or protocol-level exploit demonstrated; fixed short deadline mostly affects execution reliability/reverts.

rejected_candidates.title: AccountEncoder.decompressAccounts Lacks Bounds Checking
rejected_candidates.reason: Primarily malformed-input/self-DoS behavior; no clear permissionless fund-loss path established.

rejected_candidates.title: SwapDataHelperLib Uses Unchecked Arithmetic Without Validation
rejected_candidates.reason: Claim is too speculative and not tied to a concrete exploit causing realistic protocol harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Zero Address Validation in setDODORouteProxy GatewaySend.sol
rejected_candidates.reason: Owner misconfiguration hardening issue only; not an external exploit vector.

rejected_candidates.title: Inconsistent CEI Pattern Between claimRefund Implementations
rejected_candidates.reason: Not a distinct issue; the actionable vulnerability is already captured as reentrancy in `GatewayTransferNative.claimRefund`.

rejected_candidates.title: Inconsistent Zero Address Validation in setEddyTreasurySafe
rejected_candidates.reason: Configuration consistency concern without a direct non-privileged attack path.

rejected_candidates.title: Unsafe Type Casting from Bytes to Address
rejected_candidates.reason: Informational pattern note; no standalone exploitable protocol harm demonstrated beyond accepted findings.

rejected_candidates.title: External ID Collision Risk in _calcExternalId
rejected_candidates.reason: No practical collision exploit shown under actual nonce progression and usage.
